description: Public confirmation by President James K. Polk and newspapers turns a regional rush into a national one.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: President Polk endorses the reports to Congress
description: Polk's official message confirms that the California reports are true and accelerates migration to the goldfields.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Forty-niners pour into California
description: Large numbers of migrants from the United States and abroad enter California in search of fortune.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: San Francisco grows explosively
description: The small port of San Francisco rapidly becomes the commercial hub of the gold-rush economy.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Mining camps spread through the Sierra foothills
description: Temporary camps and boomtowns appear across the foothills as prospectors stake claims along rivers and creeks.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: California constitutional convention meets
description: Delegates gather in Monterey to draft a state constitution as the Gold Rush drives rapid political change.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: California enters the Union
description: California is admitted as a state as part of the Compromise of 1850, a transition accelerated by the Gold Rush.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Foreign miners tax is enacted
description: California imposes a tax aimed largely at foreign miners, reflecting discrimination and competition in the goldfields.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Violence and dispossession intensify for Native Californians
description: Gold-rush settlement and militia violence accelerate the destruction of Native communities across California.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Gold output reaches a new high
description: Annual gold production surges as industrializing methods and large migrant numbers transform extraction.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Rush reaches its peak year
description: The migration and output of the Gold Rush crest around 1852 before beginning a gradual decline.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Chinese migration to the goldfields rises sharply
description: Growing numbers of migrants from China become an important part of the diverse mining population.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Hydraulic mining begins to spread
description: More capital-intensive techniques increasingly replace simple placer mining in many districts.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Sacramento and interior transport networks expand
description: Roads, river traffic, and commercial services tie the mining districts more firmly to urban markets.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Initial rush phase wanes
description: By the mid-1850s, easy surface deposits are harder to find and many independent prospectors leave or change occupations.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Mining becomes more corporate and mechanized
description: Larger firms and better-capitalized operations increasingly dominate extraction after the first rush.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Comstock Lode redirects western mining attention
description: The discovery of the Comstock Lode in Nevada helps shift speculative energy away from California's first gold rush districts.

timeline_title: Timeline of the California Gold Rush
timeline_description: Major events in the discovery of gold in California, the migration of forty-niners, the transformation of society and economy, and the fading of the first rush.
title: Hydraulic mining is sharply curtailed by court ruling
description: The Sawyer decision restricts debris-producing hydraulic mining, symbolizing the long environmental aftermath of the rush.
